adds.rationale: Pure-play rockets & spacecraft ETF; opens the 'next wave' rockets bucket with a single liquid vehicle while individual launch-economy names remain private or post-date (SpaceX private; RKLB has not yet listed). Buy-and-hold-able diversified basket at inception.

Largest liquid robotics & AI ETF; opens the 'next wave' robotics bucket. Industrial automation and factory-robot demand are accelerating as the post-COVID reshoring push and global chip shortage push manufacturers toward higher automation intensity. Diversified across industrial robotics, automation components, and adjacent AI exposure.

Largest uranium/nuclear-components ETF; opens the 'next wave' nuclear bucket cheaply. Uranium spot price has begun recovering off multi-year lows in early 2021 as utility long-term contracting picks up and SMR development pipelines (NuScale, X-energy, TerraPower) attract attention. Provides diversified exposure to the nuclear renaissance without single-name technology risk.

rationale_overall: First rebalance after profile inception. Current watchlist (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, NVDA, SPY) cleanly covers the current AI wave and broad-market beta, so no removes are warranted. With a wide cap of 24 (19 slots free), this is the right moment to open three 'next wave' buckets cheaply via diversified ETFs: ARKX (rockets & spacecraft, just-launched 2021-03-30), BOTZ (robotics), and URA (nuclear). Each ETF opens a distinct wave bucket from the profile's prioritized list, avoids the engineered-biology and solar/wind exclusions, and lets us own the option on these next waves before single-name pure-plays exist publicly (RKLB, IonQ, NuScale are all still private as of today).
